mnemonics.title: General Analysis Plan
mnemonics.content: PIN TSSS: Parameter (n) → Identify Basic Operation → Determine frequency → Set up Summation → Solve → State in Asymptotic form. This helps remember the 6-step process for analyzing non-recursive algorithms.

mnemonics.title: Loop Complexity Quick Reference
mnemonics.content: Single loop: n → Nested independent: multiply → Dependent inner: sum series → Logarithmic decrement: log n. Use this to quickly identify complexity pattern in code.

mnemonics.title: Summation Formulas
mnemonics.content: Arithmetic: Σi = n(n+1)/2 → Squares: Σi² = n(n+1)(2n+1)/6 → Geometric: Σrⁱ = (rⁿ⁺¹-1)/(r-1). These are essential for solving loop summations.

acronyms.expansion: Cost function or operation count of an algorithm as function of input size n
acronyms.usage: Used to represent the exact count of basic operations in algorithm analysis

acronyms.expansion: Time complexity function of an algorithm with input size n
acronyms.usage: Formal notation for running time; T(n) = C(n) × t where t is time per basic operation
Used for worst-case analysis; f(n) = O(g(n)) means f grows no faster than g
Used for best-case analysis; f(n) = Ω(g(n)) means f grows at least as fast as g
Used when upper and lower bounds are the same; f(n) = Θ(g(n)) means f grows at same rate as g

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a rectangular grid: for i=1 to n (rows), for j=1 to m (columns) → total cells = n × m = nm. For triangular pattern (j≤i), visualize half the rectangle → n(n+1)/2 ≈ n²/2.
Think of a tree: halving n each level gives log₂n levels. The tree height represents iterations. Each level halves the remaining work, hence logarithmic.

Arithmetic series Σi from 1 to n can be visualized as area of right triangle with base n and height n. Area ≈ n²/2, hence Θ(n²). This geometric interpretation helps understand why sum of first n naturals is quadratic.

analogies.analogy: Like measuring a city's traffic by counting cars at the busiest intersection during peak hours - the basic operation is the most significant contributor to total time.
Big-O is like saying 'my car costs at most $50,000' (upper bound), Ω is like saying 'my car costs at least $30,000' (lower bound), and Θ is like saying 'my car costs exactly $40,000' (tight bound).
Best case is like finding your keys in your pocket immediately; worst case is searching everywhere before finding them; average case is the expected effort over many searches.
